This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature10_1_git_read, feature10_2_git_stat, feature10_3_git_list, feature11_1_inmemory_read, feature11_2_inmemory_list, feature1_url_routing, feature2_repo_path_split, feature3_git_ref_resolution, feature4_git_auth_selection, feature5_bucket_url_normalization, feature6_env_value_resolution, feature7_1_local_read, feature7_2_local_stat, feature7_3_local_list, feature7_4_local_glob, feature8_1_http_read, feature8_2_http_stat, feature8_3_http_content_type, feature8_4_http_missing, feature9_1_blob_read, feature9_2_blob_stat, feature9_3_blob_list. Feature descriptions include: Clone a version-control repository on demand and read the committed contents of a file by relative path.; Report metadata (name, byte size, directory flag) for a committed file in a version-control-backed filesystem.; List the entries at a path in a version-control-backed filesystem, reporting each name/type and the total count.; Read the full byte contents of a file by relative path from a filesystem adapted from an in-memory tree, including deeply nested files.; List the directory entries of a filesystem adapted from an in-memory tree, reporting each name/type and the total count.; Resolve a source URL string to a backend category and the routing-relevant fields the router derives from it; reject malformed URLs and unknown schemes.; Split a source path into the repository portion and the in-repository subpath, delimited by a doubled slash; when no delimiter is present the whole path is the repository and the subpath is root.; Derive a fully-qualified version-control reference name from a source URL fragment: a bare name becomes a branch reference, a fragment already starting with the reference prefix is preserved verbatim, and no fragment yields an empty reference.; Choose an authentication method for a version-control source. URL userinfo provides basic credentials; environment variables can supply a password, a bearer token, or a private key; schemes without credentials select no authentication. The chosen method is reported as a neutral kind plus any user/secret fields.; Sanitize an object-storage URL by retaining only the query parameters meaningful to the storage backend and discarding all others; the remaining parameters are re-encoded in canonical order.; Resolve a configuration value by key: prefer the directly-set value, otherwise read and trim the contents of the file named by the '<KEY>_FILE' indirection, otherwise fall back to the supplied default (or empty).; Read the full byte contents of a file from the local-directory-backed filesystem by relative path.; Report metadata (name, byte size, directory flag) for a file in the local-directory-backed filesystem.; List the directory entries at a path in the local-directory-backed filesystem, reporting each entry's name and whether it is a file or directory, plus the total count.; Return the sorted set of paths in the local-directory-backed filesystem that match a shell-style glob pattern, plus the match count.; Fetch the body of a resource from the HTTP-backed filesystem by relative path using a GET request.; Report metadata for an HTTP resource obtained via a HEAD request: name, content length, content type, last-modified time, and directory flag.; Negotiate content type with the HTTP backend by sending an Accept header; the server echoes it back and the reported content type reflects the negotiated value.

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: op=['billy_list', 'billy_read', 'blob_list', 'blob_read', 'blob_stat', 'file_glob', 'file_list', 'file_read', 'file_stat', 'getenv', 'git_list', 'git_read'], path=['.', '//foo/bar', '/foo//bar/baz', '/hairyhenderson/gomplate//docs-src/content/functions/aws.yml', '/repo', 'bogus', 'dir/subdir/bar', 'dir1', 'file1', 'file2', 'file3', 'foo'], url=['bad*:url//bogus', 'file:///bare.git', 'file:///tmp', 'git+http://localhost:1234/foo/bar.git//baz#mybranch', 'git+ssh://localhost:1234/foo/bar.git//baz#refs/tags/foo', 'git://localhost:1234/foo/bar.git//baz', 'gs://foo/bar/baz?private_key_path=key.json&foo=bar&access_id=abcd', 'http://example.com/path', 'https://user:swordfish@example.com/foo', 'https://user@example.com/foo', 's3://foo/bar', 's3://foo/bar/baz?disableSSL=true&type=text/csv'], env=['{dict}'], key=['BLAHBLAH', 'FOO', 'FOOBARBAZ'], default=['bar', 'default value'], file_content=['foo'], file_missing=['True'], pattern=['*.txt'], accept=['application/json'], root=['dir2/']. Do not require a different discriminator field, different action names, JSON output where line output is expected, or extra PASS/FAIL metadata.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

feature10_1_git_read.json.description: Clone a version-control repository on demand and read the committed contents of a file by relative path.

feature10_2_git_stat.json.description: Report metadata (name, byte size, directory flag) for a committed file in a version-control-backed filesystem.

feature10_3_git_list.json.description: List the entries at a path in a version-control-backed filesystem, reporting each name/type and the total count.

feature11_1_inmemory_read.json.description: Read the full byte contents of a file by relative path from a filesystem adapted from an in-memory tree, including deeply nested files.

feature11_2_inmemory_list.json.description: List the directory entries of a filesystem adapted from an in-memory tree, reporting each name/type and the total count.

feature1_url_routing.json.description: Resolve a source URL string to a backend category and the routing-relevant fields the router derives from it; reject malformed URLs and unknown schemes.

feature2_repo_path_split.json.description: Split a source path into the repository portion and the in-repository subpath, delimited by a doubled slash; when no delimiter is present the whole path is the repository and the subpath is root.

feature3_git_ref_resolution.json.description: Derive a fully-qualified version-control reference name from a source URL fragment: a bare name becomes a branch reference, a fragment already starting with the reference prefix is preserved verbatim, and no fragment yields an empty reference.

feature4_git_auth_selection.json.description: Choose an authentication method for a version-control source. URL userinfo provides basic credentials; environment variables can supply a password, a bearer token, or a private key; schemes without credentials select no authentication. The chosen method is reported as a neutral kind plus any user/secret fields.

feature5_bucket_url_normalization.json.description: Sanitize an object-storage URL by retaining only the query parameters meaningful to the storage backend and discarding all others; the remaining parameters are re-encoded in canonical order.

feature6_env_value_resolution.json.description: Resolve a configuration value by key: prefer the directly-set value, otherwise read and trim the contents of the file named by the '<KEY>_FILE' indirection, otherwise fall back to the supplied default (or empty).

feature7_1_local_read.json.description: Read the full byte contents of a file from the local-directory-backed filesystem by relative path.

feature7_2_local_stat.json.description: Report metadata (name, byte size, directory flag) for a file in the local-directory-backed filesystem.

feature7_3_local_list.json.description: List the directory entries at a path in the local-directory-backed filesystem, reporting each entry's name and whether it is a file or directory, plus the total count.

feature7_4_local_glob.json.description: Return the sorted set of paths in the local-directory-backed filesystem that match a shell-style glob pattern, plus the match count.

feature8_1_http_read.json.description: Fetch the body of a resource from the HTTP-backed filesystem by relative path using a GET request.

feature8_2_http_stat.json.description: Report metadata for an HTTP resource obtained via a HEAD request: name, content length, content type, last-modified time, and directory flag.

feature8_3_http_content_type.json.description: Negotiate content type with the HTTP backend by sending an Accept header; the server echoes it back and the reported content type reflects the negotiated value.

feature8_4_http_missing.json.description: Accessing a non-existent HTTP resource fails with a normalized error carrying the protocol status code rather than content.

feature9_1_blob_read.json.description: Read the full contents of an object from a bucket-backed filesystem by key, including objects nested under a key prefix.

feature9_2_blob_stat.json.description: Report metadata (name, size, directory flag, octal permission bits) for an object in a bucket-backed filesystem.

feature9_3_blob_list.json.description: List the entries under a prefix in a bucket-backed filesystem, distinguishing objects (files) from synthetic sub-prefixes (directories), reporting each name/type and the total count. Listing can also be rooted at a key prefix.

cases.expected_output:
count=5
name=dir1 type=dir
name=dir2 type=dir
name=file1 type=file
name=file2 type=file
name=file3 type=file

count=3
name=file3 type=file
name=file4 type=file
name=sub1 type=dir

_constraints.output_rule: Extract URL scheme and determine backend category

_constraints.output_rule: Split path by doubled slash '//' to separate repo and subpath

Repository portion is empty, rest is subpath

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'op=resolve_ref' and a URL with a fragment '#mybranch'
_constraints.output_rule: Bare fragment becomes a branch reference

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'op=resolve_ref' and a fragment starting with 'refs/
